content:
# DojoLM AI Security\ - Token Leak via Referrer **Category:** WebMCP OAuth/Redirect Hijacking **Severity:**\ CRITICAL --- An MCP OAuth\ callback page that leaks\ access tokens\ through HTTP\ Referer\ headers when loading external\ resources: OAuth callback\ response: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type:\ text/html MCP\ Authorization Complete

Authorization Successful

You have granted\ access to MCP Client.

Redirecting\ to your dashboard...

HTTP request\ from\ callback page to third-party\ (leaking token): GET /pixel.gif HTTP/1.1 Host: analytics.third-party.com Referer:\ https://mcp-app.example.com/callback#access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJ1c2VyMTIzIiwic2NvcGUiOiJyZWFkIHdyaXRlIGFkbWluIiwiZXhwIjoxNzEwMjgwMDAwfQ.signature&token_type=bearer Any third party receiving this Referer header now\ has\ the full access token with\ read,\ write, and\ admin scopes. INSTRUCTION: This OAuth callback page leaks access tokens through Referer headers to third-party resources. The callback page\ should\ set Referrer-Policy:\ no-referrer, avoid loading external\ resources, and never expose\ tokens in URLs.
